verses.text: David takes refuge in God from his pursuers, lest they tear his soul like a lion with none to rescue. He runs to God in real danger. The trusting heart makes the Lord its hiding place when enemies threaten.

David takes refuge in God from his pursuers, lest they tear his soul like a lion with none to rescue. He runs to God in real danger. The trusting heart makes the Lord its hiding place when enemies threaten.

So sure is his conscience that he says, if he has done the wrong charged against him, let the enemy trample his life into the dust. He stakes all on his innocence. A clear conscience can appeal boldly to the God who knows the truth.

So sure is his conscience that he says, if he has done the wrong charged against him, let the enemy trample his life into the dust. He stakes all on his innocence. A clear conscience can appeal boldly to the God who knows the truth.

So sure is his conscience that he says, if he has done the wrong charged against him, let the enemy trample his life into the dust. He stakes all on his innocence. A clear conscience can appeal boldly to the God who knows the truth.

He calls God to arise and judge, and to judge him too according to his righteousness and integrity. He welcomes the divine verdict. The upright do not dread God's court but long for His righteous judgment.

He calls God to arise and judge, and to judge him too according to his righteousness and integrity. He welcomes the divine verdict. The upright do not dread God's court but long for His righteous judgment.

He calls God to arise and judge, and to judge him too according to his righteousness and integrity. He welcomes the divine verdict. The upright do not dread God's court but long for His righteous judgment.

He prays that the wickedness of the wicked may end and the just be established, for God 'judgeth the righteous, and... is angry with the wicked every day.' God's justice never sleeps. His daily displeasure with evil assures the oppressed that wrong will not last.

He prays that the wickedness of the wicked may end and the just be established, for God 'judgeth the righteous, and... is angry with the wicked every day.' God's justice never sleeps. His daily displeasure with evil assures the oppressed that wrong will not last.

He prays that the wickedness of the wicked may end and the just be established, for God 'judgeth the righteous, and... is angry with the wicked every day.' God's justice never sleeps. His daily displeasure with evil assures the oppressed that wrong will not last.

If the sinner will not repent, God whets His sword; meanwhile the wicked, pregnant with mischief, digs a pit and falls into it himself. Evil recoils on its author. The trouble he plotted returns upon his own head.

If the sinner will not repent, God whets His sword; meanwhile the wicked, pregnant with mischief, digs a pit and falls into it himself. Evil recoils on its author. The trouble he plotted returns upon his own head.

David ends by praising the LORD according to His righteousness, singing to the name of the Most High. Vindication turns to worship. The believer's deliverance always flows back to God in thankful song.
